task_completion.visual_evidence: The TopoCal application window is open with 'water_main.top' visible in the title bar or loaded in the workspace, showing the initial centerline.

task_completion.description: Create a parallel polyline offset 15.0 meters to the left
task_completion.visual_evidence: An offset tool dialog showing a distance of '15.0' and a new polyline appearing parallel to the left of the original centerline in the CAD workspace.

task_completion.description: Create a parallel polyline offset 15.0 meters to the right
task_completion.visual_evidence: An offset tool dialog showing a distance of '15.0' and a second new polyline appearing parallel to the right of the original centerline in the CAD workspace.

task_completion.description: Export the drawing as a DXF to 'easements.dxf'
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export' or 'Save As' dialog box showing the file type set to DXF and the filename 'easements.dxf' being saved in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TopoCal\', or a success message confirming the export.
A 'Save As' dialog box saving the file as 'water_main_easements.top' in the specified directory, or the TopoCal application title bar updating to reflect the new project name.

integrity.description: Agent used the TopoCal GUI to perform the tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through TopoCal menus, toolbars, and dialog boxes rather than executing terminal commands or scripts to generate the files.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine TopoCal application
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual interface must clearly be the TopoCal CAD software with a graphical workspace, not a text editor modifying project files directly.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the task by creating empty files
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent using File Explorer, PowerShell, or Command Prompt to simply create blank files named 'easements.dxf' or 'water_main_easements.top'.
